    Cell / Information for authors

    Information for authors

    This page describes our= policies and provides information that we think will be helpful to you as = you prepare manuscripts for submission and publication. If you have submitt= ed a paper and want information about the status of the paper, please log i= n to our online manuscript submission system, Editor= ial Manager (EM)  Opens in new window  . If you run into = any problems or if you have specific questions, you can always e-mail us at= cellms@cell.com or call +1 617 397 = 2800.

    =20
    Submit your paper Opens in new window=  

    About this journal

    <= div class=3D"cover-image__thumb">

    Cell publishes= findings of unusual significance in any area of experimental biology, incl= uding but not limited to cell biology, molecular biology, neuroscience, imm= unology, virology and microbiology, cancer, human genetics, systems biology= , signaling, and disease mechanisms and therapeutics. The basic criterion f= or considering papers is whether the results provide significant conceptual= advances into, or raise provocative questions and hypotheses regarding, an= interesting and important biological question. In addition to primary rese= arch articles in five formats, Cell features review and opini= on articles on recent research advances and issues of interest to its broad= readership in the leading edge section.

    Aims and scope
    Editors and staff
    Advisory board
    Contact

    Performance metrics

    • 10
      Days from submission to first decision
      The median number of days from submission to first decision= (desk accept or send the paper to review or desk reject).
    • = 28
      Days from acceptance to online publication
      For papers published online, the median number of days = from acceptance to first online publication.
    Editorial speed metrics reflect median values calculated over a calendar ye= ar. They are updated annually.

    Before you submit

    What you need to know before preparing your manuscript for submission at= Cell.

    Policies

    Please review our policies to ensure that your m= anuscript is in compliance and may proceed through the submission process a= s smoothly as possible.

    • Expectations for professional behavior

    Article types

    Check to see if the journal publishes the type o= f article you are writing, and find information about length/figure restric= tions for specific article types.

    Presubmission

    Unsure if your paper is suitable for this journal? Send us a presub= mission inquiry at cellms@cell.com<= /a>, and we'll let you know what we think in 5=E2=80=937 business days. Ple= ase include a title, an abstract, and an explanation of why your paper is s= ignificant and broadly interesting.

    Cell Press Multi-Journal Submission<= /h3>

    If you think your paper might be suitable for two or more Cell Press jou= rnals, you now have the opportunity for simultaneous consideration at multi= ple journals using Cell Press Multi-Journal Submission (MJS). With MJS, all= the participating Cell Press journals of your choice provide unified consi= deration of your manuscript via one handling editor who works closely with = you, your chosen journals=E2=80=99 editorial teams, and expert reviewers fr= om the field to help you navigate a path to publication with Cell Press. To= take advantage of this unique opportunity, please submit directly to the <= a href=3D"https://www.cell.com/multi-journal-submission">MJS site. For = more information, view the MJS information for authors and MJS FAQ pages, or reach out t= o us at multi-journal@cell.com with any questions.

    Replication studies

    Although Cell Press journals do not have a dedicated format for replicat= ion studies, we are open to considering them. They will be evaluated on a c= ase-by-case basis using the same journal-specific editorial criteria used t= o evaluate other manuscripts.

    Elsevier language editing se= rvices

    Please refer to Elsevier's language editing services Opens in new window   for more information.

    Publishing options

    Learn more about our publishing options, includi= ng the types of open access licenses we offer for this journal, compliance = with funding bodies, rights and embargoes, permission to reuse content, and= ways to share your article.

    • Publication fee

    To learn more, please visit the Cell Press open access page.

    Submit your manuscript

    Revise your manuscript

    Here are some tips about how to format your manu= script, and a list of the sections that should be included in the manuscrip= t, during the revision stage. (This information is not necessary for initia= l submission.) If you have questions about which of these guidelines apply = to your manuscript, please contact your handling editor. Optional article= templates are available to help you format your article. Please note t= hat your final submission must be in an editable format, preferably Microso= ft Word.

    Revised papers that are inactive for longer than 1 year will be automati= cally withdrawn. You can contact the journal office to request further cons= ideration.

    Format

    Here are some helpful tips about how to format t= he content of your manuscript during the revision stage. (This information = is not necessary for the initial submission of your manuscript.)

    • Writing and style
      • =20
    • Math formulae and equations
      • =20

    Components to include

    These guidelines apply to most research articles= . For other formats (e.g., reviews, reports), please refer to the article t= ypes page and/or your decision letter. An optional template is available to= help you in formatting your manuscript.

    • Title
      • =20
    • Author list
      • =20
    • Author affiliations and footnotes
      • =20
    • Contact information
      • =20
    • Summary
      • =20
    • Keywords
      • =20
    • Introduction
      • =20
    • Results
      • =20
    • Limitations of the study
      • =20
    • Discussion
      • =20
    • Resource availability
      • =20
    • Acknowledgments
      • =20
    • Author contributions
      • =20
    • Declaration of interests
      • =20
    • Declaration of generative AI and AI-assisted techno= logies
      • =20
    • Supplemental information titles and legends
      • = =20
    • Figure (and/or scheme) titles and legends
      • =20
    • Tables
      • =20
    • STAR Methods
      • =20
    • Resource identification initiative
      • =20
    • References
      • =20

    Final submission

    Please refer to the following information when p= reparing to submit your final, production-ready manuscript files. All edito= rial and production formatting requirements will need to be met at this sta= ge. Any exceptions will be communicated to you by the editorial office.

    =
    • Final file requirements
      • =20
    • Revised manuscript
      • =20

      • =20

      • =20
    • Supplemental information
      • =20
    • Videos

    • Other components
      • =20

    Final file requirements (PDF)  

    Cover submissions

    After acceptance, you are welcome to submit potential cover images relat= ed to your manuscript. For more information, see our cover submission g= uidelines.

    Information

    • For Librarians

    The content on this site is intended for healthcare = professionals and researchers across all fields of science.

    =20
    We use cookies that are necessar= y to make our site work. We may also use additional cookies to analyze, imp= rove, and personalize our content and your digital experience. You can mana= ge your cookie preferences using theCoo= kie settings link. For more information, see ourC= ookie Policy

    C= ookie Preference Center

    We use cookies which are= necessary to make our site work. We may also use additional cookies to ana= lyse, improve and personalise our content and your digital experience. For = more information, see our Cookie Policy and the list of = Google Ad-Tech Vendors.

    You may choose not to allow some types of cookies. However, blocking some t= ypes may impact your experience of our site and the services we are able to= offer. See the different category headings below to find out more or chang= e your settings.

    Manage Consent Preferences

    <= div class=3D"ot-acc-hdr ot-always-active-group">

    Strictly Necessary Cookies

    Always active

    These cookies are necessary for the website to fun= ction and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set = in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, = such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. Y= ou can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some= parts of the site will not then work.

    =

    P= erformance Cookies

    These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we c= an measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know wh= ich pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around t= he site.

    <= /div>
    =

    Targeting Cookies

    These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising pa= rtners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your inte= rests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. If you do not allow the= se cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.
